g.data-narration: Welcome to Hyperledger Fabric! This comprehensive diagram covers enterprise blockchain fundamentals.
Hyperledger Fabric is an enterprise-grade blockchain framework that provides modularity, scalability, and security for various industries.
g.text: An open-source blockchain framework under Hyperledger project,
designed for building private, permissioned enterprise networks.

g.data-narration: Fabric has three key characteristics: modular architecture, scalability for enterprise needs, and robust security features.
The Fabric architecture consists of four main components: Peer Nodes, Orderer Nodes, Client Nodes, and Certificate Authority.
Peer nodes are the backbone of Fabric - they maintain the blockchain ledger, execute chaincode, and store transaction data.
Orderer nodes ensure transaction ordering and block creation using consensus algorithms like Solo or Kafka.
Client nodes interact with the Fabric network for transactions, while CA issues digital certificates for authentication.
Chaincode is Fabric's smart contract language - supports Go, Java, and Node.js for defining business logic.
g.text: Chaincode defines business logic in Fabric. Supported languages:

g.data-narration: The chaincode lifecycle has three phases: Install the code on peers, Instantiate with endorsement policy, and Upgrade when needed.
Chaincode execution involves submitting a proposal to peers, executing the code, collecting endorsements, and sending to orderer.
Fabric supports Solo (single orderer for dev/testing) and Kafka (distributed consensus for production) consensus algorithms.
g.text: Also: Raft consensus (Fabric v2.x) - Crash fault tolerant

g.data-narration: Fabric has diverse use cases: supply chain tracking, healthcare records management, and financial transaction processing.
Other blockchain frameworks for comparison include Ethereum (public, smart contracts) and Corda (private, financial services).
Key takeaways: Fabric architecture has peer, orderer, client nodes and CA. Chaincode uses Go/Java/Node.js. Consensus: Solo, Kafka, Raft.
g.text: Peer Nodes: Maintain ledger, execute chaincode
Chaincode: Go, Java, Node.js - Consensus: Solo, Kafka, Raft - Use Cases: Supply Chain, Healthcare, Finance

g.data-narration: For exam success: Understand architecture components, practice chaincode writing, study consensus, and review use cases thoroughly.
g.text: 1. Memorize 4 architecture components (Peer, Orderer, Client, CA)

g.data-narration: This diagram is complete. Continue to Module 5 for more blockchain frameworks like Ethereum and Corda.
g.text: Continue learning: Module covers Ethereum and Corda next
Complete Module 4: Fabric - university 2022 Scheme - CSE
